title: Records
doc_html:
§254.  Records

The Attorney General shall keep two sets of books or journals, which shall constitute the records of that office; one set shall contain a transcript of all the legal opinions given by him, and the other a statement of all the suits brought by him on behalf of the state; each set shall have an index.
doc_text:
§254.  Records
The Attorney General shall keep two sets of books or journals, which shall constitute the records of that office; one set shall contain a transcript of all the legal opinions given by him, and the other a statement of all the suits brought by him on behalf of the state; each set shall have an index.
